course_description.overview: Classic Alsatian gran fondo tackling three Vosges ballons with significant climbing, finishing at the summit of Planche des Belles Filles. Granfondo distance is 178 km with 4020 m elevation gain; mediofondo option is 93 km with 2200 m gain.[3]

key_segments.name: Planche des Belles Filles
key_segments.description: Legendary summit finish with extreme gradients in the final meters, a major Tour de France venue.

key_segments.name: Vosges Passes
key_segments.description: Beautiful southern Vosges passes featured in the route, ideal preparation for Alpine gran fondos.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Difficult terrain with longer, steeper hills across the Vosges mountains.[5]

logistics.getting_there: Start location in Ronchamp, Haute-Saône region near Belfort and Alsace. Accessible by car or train to nearby stations.
logistics.registration_details: Registration opens around January 2026; check official site les3ballons.com or Miles Republic.[1][5]
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bikes recommended for paved gran fondo routes.
logistics.local_considerations: Vosges mountains in eastern France; prepare for variable weather in June.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic Vosges gran fondo with brutal climbs and iconic summit finish.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Strong road cyclists seeking mountainous challenge and Tour de France-style climbs.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for climbers wanting preparation for bigger Alpine events.
pro_tips: Train for steep gradients on Planche des Belles Filles.
Opt for granfondo for full Vosges experience.
Check Tour de France 2026 Stage 14 overlaps for spectator vibes.
Register early as spots fill fast.
